serving.description: 1 open-face sandwich (1 slice of toast with avocado and scrambled egg topping)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one open-face sandwich consisting of one slice of toasted bread (approximately 60 g) topped with roughly 80 g of mashed or sliced avocado and approximately 80 g of scrambled egg, totalling around 220 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Avocado raw (FDC ID 171705), Scrambled egg (FDC ID 172185), White bread toasted (FDC ID 325871)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific café dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of three USDA FoodData Central entries: avocado raw (FDC 171705, ~36% of serving weight), scrambled egg (FDC 172185, ~36%), and toasted white bread (FDC 325871, ~28%), then normalised to per-100 g. Proportions reflect a typical 220 g serving: 80 g avocado, 80 g scrambled egg, 60 g toast. HPB Singapore Foodo and IMR Malaysia MyFCD do not carry a matching entry for this dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; white bread GI reference and mixed egg-avocado meal analogy
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific open-face sandwich. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant glycemic driver being toasted white bread (GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database) blended with the glycemic-dampening effect of avocado (high fat and fibre, GI effectively 0) and scrambled egg (negligible carbohydrate, GI effectively 0). Weighted composite approach: bread contributes ~60 g of the 220 g serving (~27% by weight) with ~28 g carbohydrate; avocado and egg contribute minimal carbohydrate. Fat and fibre from avocado are well-documented to reduce postprandial glycaemic response (Fiona S. Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021). Composite GI estimated at 52 (low). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 31.9) / 100 ≈ 16.6, rounded to 16.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg per 100 g; ~462 mg per serving) from bread, butter, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful of the sodium contributed by toasted bread and added salt; requesting reduced seasoning and choosing low-sodium bread can bring a single serving within a more acceptable sodium range for a blood-pressure-conscious diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~16.5 per serving) driven by toasted bread carbohydrate, partially offset by avocado fat and fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this dish occasionally as the avocado's monounsaturated fat and dietary fibre attenuate the postprandial glucose response from the bread; however, portion control of the bread component and pairing with a non-starchy vegetable side is advisable to keep the overall glycemic load within target.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Avocado provides cardioprotective monounsaturated fatty acids (~6.2 g per 100 g); saturated fat is moderate (~3.1 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high monounsaturated fat content from avocado is associated with improved LDL-cholesterol profiles; individuals with cardiovascular disease can benefit from this dish as part of a heart-healthy diet provided butter is used sparingly and total saturated fat intake across the day remains within recommended limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Avocado and Scrambled Egg Open-Face Sandwich is a contemporary café brunch dish popular in Singapore's Western-influenced café scene, including Malaysian-style coffee bars such as EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street. The dish consists of one slice of toasted bread — typically sourdough or white toast — topped with creamy mashed or sliced fresh avocado and soft, buttery scrambled eggs. The flavour profile is rich and savoury, with the mild nuttiness of avocado complementing the eggy creaminess of the scrambled eggs, often finished with cracked black pepper, sea salt, and occasionally chilli flakes or a squeeze of lemon. Texturally, the dish contrasts the crunch of toasted bread with the silky smoothness of the egg and the dense creaminess of the avocado. Nutritionally, a typical 220 g serving provides approximately 429 kcal, 17 g protein, 27 g fat (predominantly monounsaturated from avocado), and 32 g carbohydrate. The avocado contributes significant dietary fibre (~6 g per serving), potassium, vitamin E, and heart-healthy monounsaturated fatty acids. The eggs supply high-quality complete protein, choline, vitamin B12, and vitamin D. The glycemic index of the composite dish is estimated at 52 (low), as the fat and fibre from avocado attenuate the glycaemic response from the bread. Clinically, the dish is moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium from bread and seasoning, moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to the bread-derived glycemic load (~16.5 per serving), and low concern for cardiovascular disease given the favourable monounsaturated fat profile. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. The dish is not suitable for those with egg, gluten, or dairy allergies.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry (HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, USDA FoodData Central) covers this exact café dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of three USDA FoodData Central component entries (avocado raw FDC 171705, scrambled egg FDC 172185, toasted white bread FDC 325871) normalised to per-100 g. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database bread values and published evidence on avocado's glycaemic-dampening effect. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries; no single approved-source record exists for this specific dish. Values should be reviewed if a direct HPB or IMR Malaysia entry becomes available. 2. Serving size of 220 g is an estimate based on typical café portion (60 g toast + 80 g avocado + 80 g scrambled egg); actual restaurant portion at EC Coffee Bar may differ. 3. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not -1 sentinel values; they are composite estimates. 5. food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite sandwich dish. 6. cuisine_tags from input (Malaysian, Coffee, Cake) are noted; the dish is classified as Malaysian-influenced Western café cuisine served in Singapore. 7. No -1 sentinel values are present in per_100g; all micronutrient fields are populated with composite USDA estimates.
